purpose: Stage Two depth + traceability evidence: a real multi-artifact disk Case on the NIST CFReDS Hacking Case (SCHARDT.dd). Derived summary + trace only; no raw evidence committed.
depth_note: One disk image corroborated across 6 parsed artifact classes (custody, disk/filesystem, mft, prefetch, registry, unknown_tool_output); cross-artifact findings satisfy the >=2-class execution rule. Memory/network/evtx honestly marked not_supplied (single-evidence run).

timeline_note.behavior: The timeline artifact class is marked partial: the timeline was built primarily from mft_timeline (5000 MFT records); plaso_parse ran once and contributed a corroborating Recycle Bin staging finding (f-B-recyclebin-staging). No full plaso super-timeline was produced, so the timeline class is sealed PARTIAL rather than parsed. This is a real single-evidence disk Case; the organic tool-failure self-correction is demonstrated separately in natural-self-correction-summary.json (registry_query on truncated RegBack hives), not here.

how_to_verify: scripts/trace-finding <run-dir>  ->  'audit chain OK ... 244 leaves all resolve ... 27 findings traced'.  See nist-schardt-disk-trace.txt for the captured run.
honesty: EXPERT_REVIEW_DRAFT: not customer-ready without sigstore identity signer + human expert approval; ATT&CK blind spots and analysis limitations kept visible.
